name: Bavaria (Germany, state flag)

colors.symbolism: the heraldic blue of the House of Wittelsbach
the gold of the heraldic crown and lion

description: A horizontal white-over-blue bicolour with the Bavarian state coat of arms (Grosses Staatswappen) at the centre.
history: The Bavarian state flag with arms is reserved for use by the State Government and Landtag; the coat of arms in its current form was adopted on 5 June 1950, while the white-and-blue colour scheme dates from the 1230s.

features.symbolism: the historic colours of the House of Wittelsbach

features.role: Bavarian state coat of arms (Grosses Staatswappen)
features.symbolism: quartered shield with the Wittelsbach lozenges, the Franconian rake, the Palatine lion and the lions of Old Bavaria, surmounted by the people's crown (Volkskrone)
